steps.title: Recognize the perfect square constraint
steps.content: The left side of the equation is $(x-1)^2$, a perfect square. For any real number $x$, a perfect square is always non-negative: $(x-1)^2 \geq 0$. However, the equation requires $(x-1)^2 = -4$, which is negative.

steps.title: Conclude no solutions exist
steps.content: A non-negative quantity cannot equal a negative number. Since $(x-1)^2 \geq 0$ but we need it to equal $-4$, this is impossible. There are no real solutions. That matches choice D — D is correct.
